title: Discovered Checks
subtitle: Move one piece, reveal another
summary: Learn how a moving piece can uncover a line attack and create a forcing move.
attribution: Original prose; ideas after Nimzowitsch, My System. No book text reproduced.

steps.title: The hidden rook
steps.markdown: A discovered check appears when one piece moves away and uncovers a line to the king. The knight on e2 is blocking the rook on e1, while Black's queen sits exposed on h5.

steps.title: Reveal the check
steps.markdown: Move the knight away with tempo. The rook will give check up the e-file, and the knight can hit the queen.

hints.text: The knight must leave the e-file.
Nf4+ reveals the rook and attacks the queen on h5.

feedback.correct: Good. The knight moved, the rook gave check, and the knight also attacked the queen.
feedback.wrong: Move the knight off the e-file so the rook can see the king.

steps.title: Why discovered checks matter
steps.markdown: Why are discovered checks so dangerous?

options.label: The opponent must answer the check
options.explain: Exactly. The moving piece can attack something while the uncovered piece forces the king to respond.

options.label: The checking side gets two turns forever
options.explain: There is no extra turn. The power comes from making one move do two jobs.

options.label: Only knights can make discovered checks
options.explain: Any piece can move out of the way. The hidden checking piece is usually a rook, bishop, or queen.

steps.title: One move, two jobs
steps.markdown: Look for pieces lined up with the enemy king. If one of your own pieces blocks the line, moving it may create a forcing discovered check while the moving piece attacks something else.
